title: Indian Railways registers growth in freight, passenger services
content: The Railways loaded 142.21 million tonnes of freight during June 2026, compared with 136.71 million tonnes in June 2025; freight earnings generated an additional revenue of about ₹430 crore

title: Maharashtra government stays RTI Rules two days before Anna Hazare’s agitation
content: RTI activists demand public consultation, scrapping of the amendments, and transparency before taking further decision

title: India should not chase costly LLM race; instead, it should focus on practical AI models: Mohandas Pai
content: He was speaking at the launch of fintech firm ToneTag’s voice-first merchant banking platform eKosha here on Wednesday.

title: West Bengal Cabinet approves panel to examine draft Uniform Civil Code Bill
content: Minister Agnimitra Paul says the committee headed by retired Supreme Court judge Ranjana Prakash Desai will have four weeks to scrutinise the draft legislation and submit its recommendations

title: First batch of Amarnath pilgrims flagged off from Jammu
content: The first batch of more than 4,800 pilgrims has set off on the holy yatra in a convoy of 259 light and heavy vehicles, officials say; security on the yatra routes has been stepped up

title: Madhya Pradesh judge faces mob fury as suo motu case to protect judicial officers sits mute in Supreme Court
content: Supreme Court Advocates on Record Association slams recent threats, targeted social media campaign against MP judge; 2021 case to safeguard judges, courts was last posted for hearing in March 2025

title: Assam labourer shows 15 documents but fails citizenship test in High Court
content: The documents Aminul Hoque, a daily-wage labourer, submitted included a 1973 land purchase deed, a school certificate, and a copy of the 1951 National Register of Citizens

title: Accused cannot claim default bail for non-filing of charge sheet copies: Supreme Court judgment
content: Default bail could be claimed only if the charge sheet itself was not filed within the statutory 60 or 90 days of investigation, the judgment notes

title: Labour Ministry makes EPF contributions beyond ₹1,800 voluntary
content: Under the old scheme, the Employees' Provident Funds Scheme 1952, the wage ceiling, which is ₹15,000 per month at present, was relevant at the time of joining a firm for providing the employee with mandatory social security cover.

title: Wish I had an antidote to insecurities, says Manish Tewari after Punjab Congress revamp
content: Mr. Tewari, the Chandigarh MP, shared on X The Hindu’s report on the Congress’s Punjab revamp, which said he had been ‘left in the cold’ as the party announced new working presidents and election panels ahead of 2027 Assembly polls

title: Tree felling on CHR land in Idukki: Forest department will ask Collector to take action
content: Forest department will ask District Collector and Devikulam Subcollector to  take action against owner of Gudanpara estate for repeatedly violating lease conditions of cardamom title deeds on Cardamom Hills Reserve

title: No need to doubt transparency of SIR in Jharkhand, says CEO
content: K. Ravi Kumar says Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls is to ensure constitutional rights to Indian citizens;  foreign nationals living legally or illegally cannot participate and giving a false declaration is a criminal offence

title: Ex-CAPF officials demand withdrawal of transfers, suspension of officers whose families protested CAPF Act
content: The retired officials also claim that they are being followed and that unidentified people are filming their homes; they further stress legal right to protest

title: Farmers demand to control foam in Krishnagiri’s Kelavarapalli Dam
content: In June 2026, following rain in Karnataka, inflow into the Kelavarapalli Dam increased and foam has been accumulated in the dam since then.

title: 191 Andhra Loyola students secure paid internships
content: An eight-day Employability Readiness Training Programme conducted by Magic Bus India Foundation culminated in a campus internship drive

title: Bridge built in 1932 collapses at Penamaluru near Vijayawada, no one hurt
content: MLA attributes the collapse to age-related deterioration; no one suffered injuries as a portion of the bridge collapsed in the early hours of Thursday, say citizens

title: ED attaches ₹58.34-crore assets in Bhushan Steel case
content: The Enforcement Directorate probe is based on a criminal complaint filed by Serious Fraud Investigation Office against BSL, Neeraj Singal and others

title: Seven killed as boulder collapses at stone quarry on outskirts of Bengaluru
content: The seven deceased include five workers from M.P., and one each from Chhattisgarh and Karnataka; owners and supervisors of adjacent quarries charged with culpable homicide not amounting to murder

title: Kuki village torched in Manipur’s Noney district
content: This follows similar attacks on Kuki and Naga villages in Kamjong district, near the India-Myanmar border, on July 1

title: ECI asks both factions of TMC to submit responses on organisational elections
content: Rebel faction meets a full Bench of the EC; declining to disclose the documents submitted to the poll body, Ritabrata Banerjee says they ‘abided by all rules’ in conducting an organisational session on June 22

title: Indian Navy foils piracy attempt in Gulf of Aden; three ships remain in captivity, IMO warns
content: INS Trikand intercepts and secures Thoothukudi-bound bulk carrier Golden Arsenal; International Maritime Organization says three other ships are still held by pirates, with situation aboard one of them, which has an Indian crew member, critical; five Indians among crews of two of the three ships

title: Most TNEA toppers opt for computer science course in government school quota this year
content: PSG College of Technology, Coimbatore, emerges the preferred destination for at least half of the toppers

title: Rabri Devi gives up official bungalow, moves to own house in Patna
content: Rabri Devi has been allotted a bungalow on 39, Hardinge Road, Patna, where renovation work is on for the convenience of her ailing husband Lalu Prasad

title: FRC can fix fees only for prescribed MBBS course duration, Kerala High Court told
content: The submission came in response to a petition by the Kerala Private Medical College Managements’ Association

title: Rajasthan Royals get major relief as HC removes key hurdle to IPL in Jaipur
content: Rajasthan Cricket Association's ad hoc committee has been dissolved by the High Court, paving the way for democratic elections within three months. A sole administrator has been appointed to oversee this process. This judicial intervention is expected to revive stalled projects like the Chonp stadium and secure the Rajasthan Royals' IPL home matches in Jaipur, addressing long-standing administrative and financial disputes.

title: India's trains face a fresh wave of threats — and the red flags are everywhere
content: India's railway network faces escalating security threats, from widespread stone-pelting incidents causing significant damage to premium trains, to track sabotage and even improvised explosives. These acts, driven by mischief, frustration, and criminal intent, are testing the rail security apparatus. Authorities are deploying advanced technology like drones and solar cameras, alongside community outreach, to combat these challenges and ensure passenger safety.

title: Elon Musk’s strong clarification on reports that SpaceX showcased AI device to investors
content: Elon Musk has vehemently denied reports suggesting SpaceX is developing a physical AI device, calling the claims "Utterly False." The Wall Street Journal had reported on an early prototype of a slim handheld AI device, powered by xAI technology, intended to bypass app store restrictions.  Musk has previously expressed strong reservations about entering the phone manufacturing market, reiterating his stance against such ventures.

title: 'Only those who deliver will travel': Sports min on Asian Games selection policy
content: With 2026 Asian Games qualification process nearing its end, Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya made clear that only those teams and athletes who can bring laurels to the country will be green lit. The extensive personal support staff will not be approved at the public's expense.

title: 17-year-old strangles wife over 'repeated taunts', dumps body with father's help
content: A 17-year-old boy has been detained for allegedly murdering his 20-year-old wife in Prayagraj. He reportedly confessed to strangling her on June 11 after she taunted him. His father allegedly helped dispose of the body in Fatehpur. The woman's father had filed a missing person report after failing to contact his daughter for two days. Police are searching for the parents.

title: After Meta layoffs, Zuckerberg says firms should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: 'Enough is enough': Ex-England captain demands McCullum resign after Stokes retirement
content: Michael Vaughan has launched a fierce critique of England's cricket leadership, demanding the resignations of coach Brendon McCullum and managing director Rob Key following Ben Stokes' international retirement.  Vaughan expressed deep dissatisfaction with the team's dismal recent Test record, stating English cricket must strive for better than its current seventh-place ranking. He believes sweeping changes are necessary to revive the team's fortunes and restore pride.

title: Palantir CEO to companies using AI in 9-point note: Do not hand your data to LLM
content: Palantir has issued a stern warning to corporations regarding AI, urging them to retain proprietary data and avoid outsourcing critical operations to commercial LLM providers. The company's nine-point manifesto emphasizes "AI sovereignty," cautioning against "tokenmaxxing" – excessive spending on AI processing without a clear strategy. Palantir argues that controlling data and model "weights" is crucial for institutional future and competitive advantage, advising against decisions driven by politics or favoritism.

title: 17-year-old girl electrocuted near apartment gate in Thane; power officials booked
content: Tragedy struck Thane as a 17-year-old girl died from electric shock in a waterlogged area outside her Mumbra home. Her father alleges Torrent Power ignored prior complaints about voltage issues. Separately, a Bhiwandi sweet shop worker was electrocuted by a live wire near a temple, sparking protests over alleged negligence and attempts to hide the faulty wiring. Investigations are underway for both incidents.

title: Gauahar defends Zaid Darbar: Slams Nikhil Chinapa over alliance ‘gameplay’; viewers back Zaid
content: Gauahar Khan passionately defended Zaid Darbar when confronting Nikhil Chinapa's critique of the game. She accused Chinapa of misinterpreting the events and being politically motivated, while praising Zaid for his honesty and commitment to resolving conflicts openly. Khan challenged Chinapa's own credibility, raising concerns about his inconsistent judgments and sparking lively discussion among viewers regarding Zaid's transparent approach in the game.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: 49ers, Cowboys linked to Browns' $4.65M QB
content: The Cleveland Browns’ star player Shedeur Sanders has been a popular name concerning trade rumors ahead of the new season, sparking concern about his future. Amid all the teams that have been linked to him, it seems the Dallas Cowboys and the San Francisco 49ers have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: Alex Ovechkin's NHL future in doubt after final-game claim by insider
content: Alex Ovechkin has yet to confirm whether he will return for another NHL season, but recent developments suggest retirement is becoming increasingly likely. TSN insider Darren Dreger believes Washington's offseason moves indicate the Capitals expect their captain not to return. Ovechkin has said family, health and the team's Stanley Cup outlook will guide his decision, while head coach Spencer Carbery and general manager Chris Patrick maintain that no final choice has been made. As the organization waits for an answer, attention remains fixed on what could be the closing chapter of one of the NHL's greatest careers.

title: NHL Trade Rumor: Penguins, Rangers linked to $120M Stars scorer
content: Jason Robertson’s contract negotiations with the Dallas Stars are ongoing, and arbitration is a real possibility. Teams like the Pittsburgh Penguins and New York Rangers appear to be interested in the uncertainty, while Dallas is still looking for ways to keep one of the NHL’s top offensive talents before the 2026-27 season.

title: Telegram, Signal under Centre's scanner over username feature after WhatsApp
content: Indian authorities are ramping up their scrutiny of messaging apps, questioning both Telegram and Signal about their unique username features. This move mirrors previous discussions with WhatsApp concerning alarming trends in online fraud and impersonation. The government is keen on understanding how these platforms ensure user safety and traceability, prompting Telegram to clarify its current functionalities regarding username use.

title: Which faction is 'real' TMC? EC seeks claims, counterclaims from rival camps by July 6
content: The Election Commission has asked rival Trinamool Congress factions to submit claims regarding party signatories and organizational elections by July 6. This follows a meeting where rebel leader Ritabrata Banerjee presented his group's case, asserting they represent the "real" TMC with significant MLA support. The Mamata Banerjee camp, however, questioned the EC's decision to meet the rebels, alleging procedural violations and political influence.

title: 'What's our tolerance?': Aus ready to risk Ellyse Perry's fitness for T20 WC final
content: Perry retired hurt during Australia's semi-final win over West Indies at The Oval after feeling what the team described as "minor quad awareness". She walked off after facing seven balls, with Australia later saying the decision was taken as a precaution because they were in control of the match.

title: US pilot 'killed', plane torched in Papua; rebels call attack a 'message' to America & Indonesia
content: Separatist rebels in Papua claim to have killed an American pilot and burned a civilian plane, alleging it ferried Indonesian military personnel. The West Papua National Liberation Army stated the attack was a message to the US and Indonesian governments. Authorities confirmed the aircraft was found burnt but could not immediately verify the rebels' claims or the pilot's death. This incident escalates the ongoing low-level insurgency in the region.

title: No doctor's note, no sick leave? Germany plans major workplace shake-up
content: Under Chancellor Friedrich Merz, the government has rolled out a transformative economic reform initiative intended to enhance Germany's financial landscape. The plan encompasses significant tax cuts for families, adjustments in the pension system that raise the retirement age, and new sick leave policies that require doctor’s notes from day one.

title: New EPF Scheme 2026: What it means for your provident fund savings
content: India’s provident fund framework has now entered a new phase with the notification of the Employees’ Provident Funds Scheme, 2026 (EPF Scheme, 2026) under the Code on Social Security, 2020.

title: Rs 25 lakh a month for this? Sreejesh blasts India coach after Pro League flop
content: India ended its Pro League campaign with a 3-2 shootout win over England after a goalless draw in London on Sunday. The result helped India finish eighth with 19 points from 16 matches, after winning only four games during the season. Only Pakistan finished below them in the nine-team table.

title: Dulquer Salmaan to be summoned again by customs in luxury car smuggling case
content: Customs officials are set to question Malayalam actor Dulquer Salmaan again in connection with an alleged luxury car smuggling network. Following a seven-hour initial interrogation, investigators are reviewing his statements and seeking further documentation. The probe, dubbed 'Operation Numkhor,' is examining the illegal import of vehicles through the Bhutan border.

title: Harry Kane scripts history, becomes only second player after Ronaldo to ...
content: England found themselves behind early after Brian Cipenka gave DR Congo the lead in the seventh minute. Congo controlled large parts of the game before Kane turned the match around with goals in the 75th and 86th minutes to seal England's place in the next round.

title: Tom Cruise’s quote on ambition: Why success comes from action, not expectation
content: Hollywood icon Tom Cruise, known for his daring stunts and the 'Mission: Impossible' franchise, embodies his own powerful philosophy: 'In this life, it's not what you hope for, it's not what you deserve, it's what you take!' This mantra, from his role in 'Magnolia,' highlights the importance of proactive action and seizing opportunities to achieve dreams, a principle Cruise himself has lived by since his early days pursuing acting.

title: IMD issues heavy rain alert for Delhi-NCR: Doctor shares how to stay healthy as weather changes
content: Delhi's sudden shift from intense heat to monsoon rains has triggered health concerns. Doctors warn of increased viral infections, worsened respiratory issues due to humidity, and a heightened risk of mosquito and water-borne diseases like dengue and typhoid. Vulnerable groups, including children and the elderly, need extra caution. Practical advice includes staying hydrated, maintaining hygiene, and eliminating standing water to combat these health challenges.

title: SVAMITVA property cards: How drone surveys are changing rural land ownership
content: Drones are transforming land ownership in rural India via the SVAMITVA scheme, which efficiently maps residential areas and provides legal ownership cards. With over 3.29 lakh villages surveyed, millions of property cards are now distributed. This initiative aims to minimize disputes, establish proprietary rights, and assist in rural planning, with a focus on promoting co-ownership for women, while agricultural lands remain excluded.

title: 'Vaibhav will do very well, but...': Karthik settles Samson vs Sooryavanshi debate
content: Dinesh Karthik backs Sanju Samson despite recent struggles, urging continued faith in the experienced batter over rising star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. Karthik highlighted Samson's past T20 World Cup performance as a reason for backing. He also commented on India's decision to bat first against England, attributing it to an 'Ireland hangover'. Captain Shreyas Iyer's crucial half-century was praised for relieving pressure.

title: 'When legal route fails ... ': BJP links Priyanka Gandhi to Uttarakhand 'land-grab' bid
content: The BJP has accused Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and her husband Robert Vadra of involvement in an alleged land grab attempt in Uttarakhand. Party spokesperson Pradeep Bhandari claimed Robert Vadra's sister-in-law, Saira Vadra, is trying to acquire a four-acre property. He alleged a Congress MLA and supporters intimidated the 90-year-old occupant, Nasreen Khan, to vacate the land, calling it the "Gandhi-Vadra model of land-grabbing."

title: 4 BMC officials suspended after 60-year-old dies in storm drain
content: A 60-year-old man tragically lost his life after falling into an open manhole during drain maintenance in Sakinaka. The incident has led to the suspension of four BMC officials pending an inquiry. Eyewitnesses reported the man fell while talking on his phone, with rescue efforts proving unsuccessful. The tragedy has ignited widespread outrage and calls for accountability, with political leaders demanding strict action and compensation for the victim's family.

title: Ronaldo's Portugal vow to 'honour Diogo Jota' on first death anniversary
content: Portugal's World Cup journey intensifies as they face Croatia in the Round of 32, carrying a profound motivation. The match falls on the first anniversary of the tragic deaths of Diogo Jota and André Silva.  Jota, a key forward and Nations League winner, is remembered as an inspiration, with the squad aiming to win the World Cup in his honor, fulfilling his unachieved dream.

title: ‘Baaten Hain, Baaton Ka Kya’: Why India-Pakistan Track II, III, IV... diplomacy is a song on loop
content: A recent open letter from prominent Indian and Pakistani citizens urging renewed dialogue for peace faces an uphill battle. The article argues that India-Pakistan relations are stalled not by a lack of meetings, but by deep-seated ideological, political, and institutional divides. Pakistan's military establishment holds the real power, rendering civilian-led talks potentially futile.

title: Air India cuts fuel surcharge on its North America, UK, EU & Australia by upto 39%
content: Air India has significantly reduced its fuel surcharge on long-haul flights to North America, the UK, Europe, and Australia, with reductions of up to $80 on some routes. This adjustment follows a dip in crude oil prices. Meanwhile, its low-cost arm, Air India Express, has fully restored connectivity across its West Asia network, resuming services to Oman and Kuwait.

title: Woman stripped, paraded with shoe garland by live-in partner’s family in Ujjain
content: A 30-year-old woman faced brutal public humiliation in Ujjain's Jharda area after resuming a live-in relationship with a man recently released from jail. Her clothes were torn, face blackened, hair cut, and she was paraded with a shoe garland by her partner's father, relatives, and villagers. Police have registered a case against 11 accused and arrested three, citing anger over her return after a prior rape case.

title: 'You've to earn': Ex-India cricketer says Sooryavanshi shouldn't replace Samson
content: Sooryavanshi has travelled with the Indian squad for the T20I series against Ireland and England but is yet to make his international debut. India have instead continued to back the top-order trio of Sanju Samson, Abhishek Sharma and Ishan Kishan, all of whom played important roles in India's 2026 T20 World Cup triumph.

title: Public Accounts Committee chair pulls up Education Secretary over remarks on CBSE queries
content: After the Secretary tries to prevent queries to CBSE, PAC chair K.C. Venugopal says officials cannot dictate what Parliament panels can discuss; Secretary withdraws comments after objections, including from BJP MPs

title: DMK is not accepting defeat, says Shanmugam
content: The DMK tried forming a government jointly with the AIADMK and many in the DMK and the AIADMK also opposed it, he says

title: Epidemic prevention action plan to be launched in Ernakulam
content: Minister for Higher Education Roji M. John will launch the year-long programme, which will be jointly implemented by the district panchayat and the Health department

title: ED recovers documents in raids on firm in T.N., Kerala
content: The searches, conducted by the ED’s Chennai zonal office under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act on June 30, covered premises in Coimbatore, Krishnagiri, Malappuram, and Thrissur

title: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu lashes out at Jagan for changing his tune on State capital
content: From supporting Amaravati to mooting three capitals, and now chanting MAVIGUN, the YSRCP chief has been inconsistent on the issue, he says, asserting that no force on earth can stall development of Amaravati

title: Development must be measured by its impact on people’s lives, says Naidu
content: Government’s real yardstick is not merely the growth reflected in indicators such as Gross State Domestic Product, but the extent to which that growth transformed the lives of the poor, asserts Chief Minister

title: Applications invited for A.P. State Best Teacher Awards–2026
content: Government school teachers can submit applications to the respective Deputy Educational Officers of their divisions by July 15

title: Missing for 12 days, five-year-old reunited after man walks into police station with child
content: Police said the hotel worker, who had been living alone after separating from his family, claimed he thought the child had been abandoned at Secunderabad Railway Station and took him home, later becoming emotionally attached to the boy.

title: Strong opposition to staging Kambala during Dasara in Mysuru
content: The participants also decided to constitute an action committee comprising representatives of the participating organisations to plan a series of protests against the event

title: Maharashtra government to set up STF to regulate activities of sonography, IVF centres
content: A law will be introduced concerning the functioning of these centres by the next session of the State Assembly, says Maharashtra Health Minister

title: "Heard Screams, Crying": Family Of 3 From Gujarat Killed In US Motel Fire
content: The victims were identified as Hiteshbhai Suthar, his wife, Hinaben, and their 20-year-old daughter, Ishani. They were trapped inside a room in the motel, a fire official told news agency Associated Press.

title: MLA Disqualified, Bypoll In Madhya Pradesh's Datia To See Prestige Fight
content: Within the Congress, intense discussions are in progress over the choice of candidate. The strongest buzz surrounds Arjun Bharti, son of disqualified former MLA Rajendra Bharti

title: Strong Indicators Show India's Economic Resilience Amid Global Uncertainties
content: Showing India's growth momentum, the nation's GDP, one of the most important economic indicators, grew by 7.7 per cent in financial year 2025-26, reaffirming its position as the world's fastest-growing major economy.

title: Kerala Man Suffers Heart Attack In Traffic. 2 Nurses Jump Off Bus, Give CPR
content: The rescue was also made possible by local residents. A passerby took over the wheel after learning that Sinoj was rushing to hospital with chest pain.

title: After WhatsApp, Centre Sends Notices To Telegram, Signal Over Usernames: Sources
content: The government is concerned that with the introduction of usernames -- meant to keep phone numbers private -- there will be a spurt in online impersonation, fraud and digital arrests.

title: "They Are Using Ethanol In Racing Cars": Oil Minister Hardeep Puri
content: Puri defended the government's push towards greater biofuel blending while addressing the heavy financial toll on oil marketing companies amid recent global crude price volatility triggered by the Middle East crisis.

title: Trees Cut, Ponds Destroyed In Key Kerala Reserve, High Court Takes Note
content: The environmental damage centres on nearly 300 acres of cardamom patta land at Goodanpara Estate, where forest officials say large-scale tree cutting and excavation were carried out under the guise of developing cardamom plantations.

title: Pak Army Handed Guns To Kashmiris, Now They Call Us Terrorists: PoK Leader
content: Sardar Aman Khan, the leader of the Joint Awami Action Committee, claimed that it is Pakistan's military that originally supplied weapons to Kashmiris.

title: India, Japan Vibrant Democracies With Shared Values: Envoy Nagma Mallick
content: The envoy said during India and Japan's bilateral engagements, "our two sides will hold discussions to deepen our economic partnership to mutual benefit".

title: After Pak, It's Bangladesh, Myanmar: China Plans New Corridor Bordering India
content: The plans about the corridor to India's east come after China got access to the Arabian Sea with the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor to the west. India strongly objects to the CPEC corridor because it runs through Gilgit-Baltistan, which is a part of Pakistan-occupied Kashmir.

title: Chinese Presence Growing In Indo-Pacific, India-Japan Ties Closer: Experts
content: In a discussion with NDTV today, India's former Ambassador to Japan Sujan R Chinoy said while China is a global power in terms of economy, it is not quite there yet in terms of military power.

title: Siya Goyal Agrees To Lie Detector Test In Ketan Agarwal Murder Probe
content: Police took Siya Goyal to a site in Pune on Thursday morning where she and her alleged lover, Chetan Chaudhary, had rehearsed how to push Ketan Agarwal off the cliff, police said.

title: Panic Button, Surveillance: Security Boost For Mumbai Locals After Stabbing
content: One of the key proposals under consideration is expanding the use of facial recognition-enabled surveillance cameras at major railway stations by replacing conventional CCTV systems.

title: Provident Fund Contribution Beyond Rs 1,800 A Month Voluntary: Centre
content: Under the new Employees' Provident Funds Scheme 2026, notified by the Labour and Employment Ministry on Monday, both employee and employer contributions will be capped at the current wage limit of Rs 15,000 per month.

title: Indonesian Couple Publicly Caned 21 Times Over TikTok Kissing Video
content: In Indonesia's Aceh province, a Sharia court sentenced an unmarried couple to public caning after their TikTok kissing video sparked outrage

title: Japan Parliament Adds 2 More Women's Toilets For 68 Female MPs
content: Japan's 1936 lower house has 22 women's stalls versus 67 for men, reflecting a political gender gap with women holding just 68 of 465 seats.

title: Cables In Drain, Unanswered Calls: 17-Year-Old Electrocuted In Rain-Hit Thane
content: The victim, Aliya Chandiwala, a resident of Yasmeen Park in Mumbra near Thane, had stepped out for some work when she was electrocuted in a waterlogged area.

title: High Court Rejects 16 Documents Presented By Man As Proof Of Citizenship
content: The court made the observation dismissing a petition challenging a lower-court order that declared an Assam resident a foreigner.

title: 5 Killed, 16 Injured As Blast Rips Through Damascus Cafe
content: The blast took place near the capital's Palace of Justice, a key government building, sparking scenes of panic in the busy area.

title: In Punjab Reset, Congress Steers Clear Of 2022 Pitfalls, Sends Unity Message
content: The appointments suggest that the Congress leadership has learnt lessons from the turmoil that had hurt the party before the 2022 assembly elections. Back then, the party had replaced Captain Amarinder Singh with Charanjit Singh Channi as Chief Minister.

title: 5 killed, 16 injured after massive blast rocks Syrian capital Damascus
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: US capital ready for biggest pyrotechnics in history — in stifling heat
content: temperatures in much of the US are forecast to hit record highs on July 4 . more than 860,000 pyrotechnic effects will light up the skies during a 40-minute finale . this year, "spectacular" is being bumped up to "supersized"

title: How Bengal is taking a 'Right' turn: What Suvendu Adhikari changed in post-Mamata era
content: Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More

title: When chemistry beats caste: A quiet revolt is rewriting the oldest rule of arranged marriage
content: defining questions today are no longer limited to surname, caste or community . they are about emotional maturity, career ambitions, financial compatibility, family expectations, mental health, parenting, equality and shared life goals . compatibility is steadily replacing community as the most valuable currency .
